primary_accounting_issue: Déterminer si le revenu d’une licence de propriété intellectuelle est constaté à une date donnée ou de façon étalée selon que la licence confère un droit d’accès ou un droit d’utilisation.

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I applied the strict precedence rule by selecting the IFRS standard that directly governs revenue recognition for licences of intellectual property; there was no overlapping lower-precedence governing document in the provided context to displace it.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: Low — the context includes specific IFRS 15 licence guidance that directly addresses the timing of revenue recognition for intellectual property licences.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern how to determine the nature of a licence promise and whether revenue is recognised over time or at a point in time.

supporting_authority.reason: These paragraphs provide the general control-based framework and the over-time versus point-in-time distinction that the licence-specific guidance applies.
These paragraphs support the over-time model by governing how progress is measured once a licence is identified as a right to access.
These paragraphs provide a royalty-specific overlay relevant to licence contracts, but they modify timing within the licence model rather than create a separate peer approach.

peripheral_authority.reason: The real-estate-focused over-time guidance is topically related to timing of revenue but not relevant to identifying peer approaches for intellectual property licences.

These paragraphs address transaction price, variable consideration, financing, non-cash consideration, and consideration payable to a customer, which are not needed to identify the peer licence-recognition approaches.
The principal-versus-agent guidance concerns who recognises revenue gross or net, not whether a licence is recognised at a point in time or over time.

treatment_families.family: Distinct licence assessed by reference to the nature of the promise

approaches.label: Right to access
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 15 treats a licence as a right to access intellectual property when the criteria in paragraph B58 are met, leading to revenue recognition over time under paragraph B60.

approaches.label: Right to use
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 15 treats a licence as a right to use intellectual property when the criteria in paragraph B58 are not met, leading to revenue recognition at a point in time under paragraph B61.
